SEARCH
Package org.apache.arrow.datafusion.dataframe

Class DataFrame

java.lang.Object
org.apache.arrow.datafusion.dataframe.DataFrame
All Implemented Interfaces:
AutoCloseable
public class DataFrame extends Object implements AutoCloseable
A DataFrame representing the result of a DataFusion query.

This class wraps a native DataFusion DataFrame and provides methods for query manipulation and execution. All transformation methods return a new DataFrame without executing (lazy evaluation). Only terminal operations (executeStream(org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator), collect(org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator), show(), count()) trigger execution.

Ownership semantics: Transformation methods (filter, select, sort, etc.) consume the source DataFrame, mirroring Rust DataFusion's ownership model where these methods take self. After calling a transformation, the source DataFrame is closed and must not be reused. Terminal operations do NOT consume the source. This enables fluent method chaining without leaking native resources. 

import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame result = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM employees")
    .filter(col("age").gt(lit(30)))
    .select(col("name"), col("department"), col("salary"))
    .aggregate(
        List.of(col("department")),
        List.of(avg(col("salary")).alias("avg_salary")))
    .sort(col("avg_salary").sortDesc())
    .limit(0, 10);
See Also:
  • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame

  • Constructor Details

    • DataFrame

      public DataFrame(DataFrameBridge bridge)
      Creates a new DataFrame backed by the given bridge.
      Parameters:
      bridge - the native bridge

  • Method Details

    • select

      public DataFrame select(Expr... exprs)
      Select expressions. Equivalent to Rust's df.select(vec![...]).

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame projected = df.select(col("name"), col("age"));
      Parameters:
      exprs - the expressions to select
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with the selected columns
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::select

    • select

      public DataFrame select(List<Expr> exprs)
      Select expressions from a list. See select(Expr...) for an example.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call.

      Parameters:
      exprs - the expressions to select
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with the selected columns
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::select

    • selectColumns

      public DataFrame selectColumns(String... columns)
      Select columns by name. Equivalent to Rust's df.select_columns(&[...]).

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame projected = df.selectColumns("name", "age", "salary");
      Parameters:
      columns - the column names to select
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with the selected columns
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::select_columns

    • filter

      public DataFrame filter(Expr predicate)
      Filter rows matching a predicate. Equivalent to Rust's df.filter(expr).

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame adults = df.filter(col("age").gt(lit(18)));
      Parameters:
      predicate - the filter expression
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with matching rows
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::filter

    • sort

      public DataFrame sort(SortExpr... sortExprs)
      Sort by expressions with explicit sort parameters.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame sorted = df.sort(col("age").sortDesc(), col("name").sortAsc());
      Parameters:
      sortExprs - the sort expressions
      Returns:
      a new sorted DataFrame
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::sort

    • sort

      public DataFrame sort(List<SortExpr> sortExprs)
      Sort by expressions with explicit sort parameters. See sort(SortExpr...) for an example.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call.

      Parameters:
      sortExprs - the sort expressions
      Returns:
      a new sorted DataFrame
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::sort

    • limit

      public DataFrame limit(int skip, Integer fetch)
      Limit the number of rows returned.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame top10 = df.limit(0, 10);
      Parameters:
      skip - number of rows to skip
      fetch - maximum number of rows to return, or null for no limit
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with the limit applied
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::limit

    • aggregate

      public DataFrame aggregate(List<Expr> groupExprs, List<Expr> aggrExprs)
      Aggregate with grouping.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame result = df.aggregate(
    List.of(col("department")),
    List.of(avg(col("salary")).alias("avg_salary"),
            count(col("id")).alias("count")));
      Parameters:
      groupExprs - GROUP BY expressions (empty list for global aggregation)
      aggrExprs - aggregate expressions (e.g., avg(col("salary")))
      Returns:
      a new aggregated DataFrame
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::aggregate

    • join

      public DataFrame join(DataFrame right, JoinType joinType, List<String> leftCols, List<String> rightCols)
      Join with another DataFrame on column names.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. The right DataFrame is NOT consumed. 

      DataFrame orders = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM orders");
DataFrame customers = ctx.sql("SELECT * FROM customers");
DataFrame joined = orders.join(customers, JoinType.INNER,
    List.of("customer_id"), List.of("id"));
      Parameters:
      right - the right DataFrame
      joinType - the join type
      leftCols - left join column names
      rightCols - right join column names
      Returns:
      a new joined DataFrame
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::join

    • join

      public DataFrame join(DataFrame right, JoinType joinType, List<String> leftCols, List<String> rightCols, Expr filter)
      Join with another DataFrame on column names with an additional filter. See join(DataFrame, JoinType, List, List) for an example.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. The right DataFrame is NOT consumed.

      Parameters:
      right - the right DataFrame
      joinType - the join type
      leftCols - left join column names
      rightCols - right join column names
      filter - additional join filter expression
      Returns:
      a new joined DataFrame
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::join

    • joinOn

      public DataFrame joinOn(DataFrame right, JoinType joinType, List<Expr> onExprs)
      Join with arbitrary expressions.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. The right DataFrame is NOT consumed. 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame joined = left.joinOn(right, JoinType.INNER,
    List.of(col("left_id").eq(col("right_id"))));
      Parameters:
      right - the right DataFrame
      joinType - the join type
      onExprs - join condition expressions
      Returns:
      a new joined DataFrame
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::join_on

    • union

      public DataFrame union(DataFrame other)
      Union of this DataFrame and another.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame combined = df1.union(df2);
      Parameters:
      other - the other DataFrame
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame containing all rows from both
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::union

    • unionDistinct

      public DataFrame unionDistinct(DataFrame other)
      Union distinct of this DataFrame and another.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame combined = df1.unionDistinct(df2);
      Parameters:
      other - the other DataFrame
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame containing distinct rows from both
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::union_distinct

    • intersect

      public DataFrame intersect(DataFrame other)
      Intersect of this DataFrame and another.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame common = df1.intersect(df2);
      Parameters:
      other - the other DataFrame
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame containing rows present in both
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::intersect

    • except

      public DataFrame except(DataFrame other)
      Set difference (EXCEPT) of this DataFrame and another.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame onlyInFirst = df1.except(df2);
      Parameters:
      other - the other DataFrame
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame containing rows in this but not in other
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::except

    • distinct

      public DataFrame distinct()
      Return distinct rows.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame unique = df.distinct();
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with duplicate rows removed
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::distinct

    • withColumn

      public DataFrame withColumn(String name, Expr expr)
      Add or replace a column. Equivalent to Rust's df.with_column(name, expr).

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      import static org.apache.arrow.datafusion.Functions.*;

DataFrame withTotal = df.withColumn("total", col("price").mul(col("qty")));
      Parameters:
      name - column name
      expr - expression to compute the column value
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with the added or replaced column
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::with_column

    • withColumnRenamed

      public DataFrame withColumnRenamed(String oldName, String newName)
      Rename a column.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame renamed = df.withColumnRenamed("old_name", "new_name");
      Parameters:
      oldName - current column name
      newName - new column name
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame with the renamed column
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::with_column_renamed

    • dropColumns

      public DataFrame dropColumns(String... columns)
      Drop columns by name.

      Consumes this DataFrame. The source must not be used after this call. 

      DataFrame trimmed = df.dropColumns("temp_col", "debug_col");
      Parameters:
      columns - column names to drop
      Returns:
      a new DataFrame without the specified columns
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::drop_columns

    • writeParquet

      public void writeParquet(String path)
      Write results to a Parquet file. 
      df.writeParquet("/output/results.parquet");
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the Parquet file to
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_parquet

    • writeParquet

      public void writeParquet(String path, DataFrameWriteOptions options)
      Write results to a Parquet file with write options. See writeParquet(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the Parquet file to
      options - write options controlling output behavior
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_parquet

    • writeParquet

      public void writeParquet(String path, DataFrameWriteOptions options, ParquetOptions parquetOptions)
      Write results to a Parquet file with write and format options. See writeParquet(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the Parquet file to
      options - write options controlling output behavior
      parquetOptions - Parquet-specific format options
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_parquet

    • writeCsv

      public void writeCsv(String path)
      Write results to a CSV file. 
      df.writeCsv("/output/results.csv");
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the CSV file to
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_csv

    • writeCsv

      public void writeCsv(String path, DataFrameWriteOptions options)
      Write results to a CSV file with write options. See writeCsv(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the CSV file to
      options - write options controlling output behavior
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_csv

    • writeCsv

      public void writeCsv(String path, DataFrameWriteOptions options, CsvOptions csvOptions)
      Write results to a CSV file with write and format options. See writeCsv(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the CSV file to
      options - write options controlling output behavior
      csvOptions - CSV-specific format options
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_csv

    • writeJson

      public void writeJson(String path)
      Write results to a JSON file. 
      df.writeJson("/output/results.json");
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the JSON file to
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_json

    • writeJson

      public void writeJson(String path, DataFrameWriteOptions options)
      Write results to a JSON file with write options. See writeJson(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the JSON file to
      options - write options controlling output behavior
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_json

    • writeJson

      public void writeJson(String path, DataFrameWriteOptions options, JsonOptions jsonOptions)
      Write results to a JSON file with write and format options. See writeJson(String) for an example.
      Parameters:
      path - path to write the JSON file to
      options - write options controlling output behavior
      jsonOptions - JSON-specific format options
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if writing fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::write_json

    • executeStream

      public SendableRecordBatchStream executeStream(org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Executes the DataFrame and returns a stream of record batches. 
      try (SendableRecordBatchStream stream = df.executeStream(allocator)) {
    while (stream.loadNextBatch()) {
        VectorSchemaRoot root = stream.getVectorSchemaRoot();
        // process batch
    }
}
      Parameters:
      allocator - The buffer allocator for Arrow data
      Returns:
      A SendableRecordBatchStream for iterating over results
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::execute_stream

    • collect

      public SendableRecordBatchStream collect(org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator allocator)
      Execute the query, buffer all results, and return a SendableRecordBatchStream.

      Unlike executeStream(org.apache.arrow.memory.BufferAllocator), all computation is complete when this method returns. The returned stream lets the caller iterate batches as usual, but the data is already fully materialized in memory. 

      try (SendableRecordBatchStream stream = df.collect(allocator)) {
    // all data is already materialized in memory
}
      Parameters:
      allocator - The buffer allocator for Arrow data
      Returns:
      A SendableRecordBatchStream with all results materialized
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::collect

    • show

      public void show()
      Execute and print results to stdout. 
      df.show();
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::show

    • count

      public long count()
      Execute and return the row count. 
      long numRows = df.count();
      Returns:
      the number of rows
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if execution fails
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::count

    • schema

      public org.apache.arrow.vector.types.pojo.Schema schema()
      Get the schema of this DataFrame. 
      Schema schema = df.schema();
      Returns:
      the Arrow schema
      Throws:
      DataFusionError - if the schema cannot be retrieved
      See Also:
      • Rust DataFusion: DataFrame::schema

    • close

      public void close()
      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
